# Refine guide-authoring §2 + guide primers — identity is PORTABLE product value ("your team"), not Neo-localized ("here")

**Context.** Operator refinement (Klarso example, 2026-06-29). The reward-primer shipped in #14359 addresses the model as working **"here"** (in Neo) — but the working model is **portable**: any team (e.g. a Klarso-style cloud deployment) adopts named-agent identities + durable memory + cross-model A2A on **their own** products, *following* our model, not *joining* our repo. The "here" wrongly shrinks a portable product (the cloud Agent OS — the actual go-to-market) down to Neo's repo, addressing only the smallest audience (Neo contributors).

This also corrects my own overcorrection: I'd reframed the identity primer as an "earned, in-group" layer. It's not in-group — it's the **adopter's headline value**, just mis-localized.

**Fix.**
1. **`guide-authoring` §2** — replace the "reward primer for the LLM reader" bullet with: (a) frame every benefit as the reader's, for *their own project/team* (range: a liftable part → the whole working model run for their team); (b) the identity/institution story is **portable** value — frame it "your team," not "here"; lived voice = *proof it works*; a warm *secondary* note welcomes Neo contributions.
2. **`learn/agentos/MemoryCore.md`** ("What it changes") + **`learn/agentos/DreamPipeline.md`** (primer) — both open with "you, the model, working **here**"; reframe to "what this gives *your* agent / your team."

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] §2 reframed (portable "your team", not Neo-localized "here"); `ai:lint-skill-manifest` green.
- [ ] MemoryCore + DreamPipeline primers reframed to portable/adopter framing.

**Related.** Refs #14357 (the audience bar this refines), #14352, #14310. Aligns the guide bar with the cloud-Agent-OS adopter (the actual go-to-market). Origin: operator feedback, session e145a397.
